content.full_text:
BRUNICK ALVAREZ & BATTERSBY
A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION
1839 COMMERCENTER WEST
POST OFFICE BOX 6425
SAN BERNARDINO, CALIFORNIA 92412
TELEPHONE: AREA CODE 714
889-8301 824-0623

WILLIAM J. BRUNICK
DONALD B. ALVAREZ
MARGUERITE P. BATTERSBY

KAREN BOEGLER MCHUGH
JAMES W. ANDERSON
DONALD F. CASH
STEVEN M. KENNEDY
FRANCES G. HERBERT
BETTY S. N. AUTON

215 CAJON STREET
P.O. BOX 1320
REDLANDS, CALIFORNIA 92373
TELEPHONE (714) 793-0818

PLEASE REFER TO

16-0032

January 7, 1991


FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: Request for Advice pursuant to Government Code
Section 83114(b)

To the Commission:

On behalf of City Councilman Edward Henderson, I am requesting
advice regarding his duties and responsibilities under the
conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.
The facts which we believe pertinent to this request are as
follows:

FACTS

The Councilman has a recorded joint tenancy interest, with a
right of survivorship, in a mobilehome which is located in a
mobilehome park in the City. The other joint tenant is the
Councilman's elderly aunt. The Councilman's aunt resided in
the mobilehome until she became ill approximately two years ago
and hopes to return to the mobilehome when her health has
improved. The Councilman occupied the home temporarily to
"house sit" when she left to obtain medical care. The
Councilman returned to the Councilman's own home a few months
ago and the mobilehome is now vacant. The Councilman did not
sign any lease or rental agreement with the mobilehome park or
the aunt for the mobilehome park space. The rent for the
mobilehome park space is billed solely to the Councilman's aunt
and is approximately $210 per month. The Councilman handles
the aunt's finances and writes the checks for the rent from a
checking account in which the Councilman and the aunt are joint
tenants, but which contains only the aunt's funds.

Fair Political Practices Commission
January 7, 1991
Page 2




In June, 1990, the City Council adopted an interim urgency rent
control ordinance to regulate the amount that owners of
mobilehome parks may charge tenants to rent spaces in
mobilehome parks, which was to expire December 31, 1990. On
December 17, 1990, the City Council adopted, by Interim Urgency
Ordinance, and conducted its first reading of a permanent rent
control ordinance.  Under the new ordinance, rents will be
"rolled-back" to those in effect on December 31, 1988, plus any
increases imposed between that date and the effective date of
the Ordinance which do not exceed 66.67% of the increase in the
Consumer Price Index between that date and the effective date
of the Ordinance. Rents may automatically be increased once
every twelve months by 66.67% of the annual change in the
Consumer Price Index.  Additional increases are available by
administrative application and hearing. Under the new ordinance
rents may also be increased in an unregulated amount when
ownership of a home is transferred or a home is removed from a
park. This is known as "vacancy decontrol."

There are currently 43 mobilehome parks within the City
containing a total of approximately 4,104 rented spaces. This
represents approximately 30% of the residential units within
the City of Yucaipa.  The vacancy rate in these mobilehome
parks is virtually zero.  The total number of residential
dwelling units in the City, including mobilehome spaces, is
approximately 13,483. These figures were obtained by the City
from a report by the San Bernardino County Assessor dated
January of 1990. The population of the City is estimated to be
32,000.  The City has no figures for the total number of
persons residing in mobilehome parks.

Councilman Henderson has excused himself from participation in
the adoption of the mobilehome rent control ordinance which is
currently in effect in the City. However, he wishes to obtain
an opinion as to his ability to participate in future
mobilehome rent control matters.

Prior to the Council's most recent action on mobilehome rent
control, an opinion as to Councilman Henderson's ability to
participate in the decisionmaking was requested from the City's
special counsel.  This opinion was sought in the interest of
time, with the knowledge that the Commission would not have
time to adequately review the issue prior to the Council's
intended action.  A copy of the opinion letter of Richards,
Watson & Gershon is attached for your review.

Fair Political Practices Commission
January 7, 1991
Page 3




Please let me know if I can be of further assistance.

                                                Yours sincerely,

                                                BRUNICK, ALVAREZ & BATTERSBY

                                                [signature]

                                                Marguerite P. Battersby
                                                Interim City Attorney
                                                CITY OF YUCAIPA


MPB:prc
Enclosure
cc: Leslie Keane Stratton, City Manager
    Ed Henderson, Councilman
    Mayor and Council
    CITY OF YUCAIPA

sections.question_synthetic: Can City Councilman Edward Henderson participate in future mobilehome rent control matters given his joint tenancy interest in a mobilehome located in a mobilehome park within the city, where he has no direct financial stake but holds property rights with his elderly aunt?
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: This is an incoming advice request, not an FPPC response letter. The document contains the FACTS section describing the councilman's situation and the mobilehome rent control context, but no QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are explicitly labeled. The legal question is synthesized from the request's stated purpose. Document references a prior opinion from Richards, Watson & Gershon (enclosure).

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from the City of Yucaipa's interim city attorney seeking FPPC advice on whether City Councilman Edward Henderson can participate in future mobilehome rent control ordinance decisions, given his recorded joint tenancy interest in a mobilehome in the city.
